title: Sen. Klobuchar questions Noem on Minnesota ICE operations, Renee Good and Alex Pretti killings
author: CBS News
fullText: Senator Globach. >> Thank you, Mr. Chairman. Secretary Gnome, as Secretary of Homeland Security, you oversaw Operation Metro Search. Is that correct? >> Uh, yes. As Secretary, I oversee ICE and CBP and HSI officers that were there doing their work. >> 3,000 ICE agents border control to Minnesota, outnumbering our 10 metro police out departments, outnumbering by 3 to one. uh the number of sworn police officers in Minneapolis and St. Paul. Is that correct? >> We were working to do targeted operations against criminal illegal aliens and we needed to do that in a safe manner to make sure that we could ensure the safety of our law enforcement officers. >> Two of my constituents, Renee Good and Alex Prey were killed. Uh they should be alive today. In fact, in one month in the city of Minneapolis, when you look at the three fatalities that were results of shooting, two of three were committed by federal agents. Are you aware of that? >> Yes, I am. So your agents not only their actions resulted in the deaths of two innocent American citizens, but they repeatedly violated my constituents first amendment rights to assemble. You say you believe in the Second Amendment right to bear arms, but Alex Prey was criticized repeatedly by officials in the administration for having a lawful permit to carry and having a gun. Your agents violated the fourth amendment rights of my constit my constituents by ramming through doors of innocent people's homes innocent citizens home without any kind of a warrant and violated the fifth amendment right to due process. So as I have shared with my colleagues if you believe in federalism in freedom and in liberty you should be horrified by what the department of homeland security did in Minnesota. So my first question is having spoken to Mr. Hman, what is the exact number of DHS agents still in Minnesota? >> Well, I believe that there is uh still close to 650 there counting the investigators that are there uh working to get to the bottom of the unprecedented fraud that has been found in the Medicaid >> funy Liam involved in that fraud. As you know, I am all in on prosecuting fraud. I put in place the US attorney who exposed the fraud under the Biden administration and brought the bulk of the prosecutions and also recommended to Mr. Blanch that Joe Thompson be the acting US attorney who led those prosecutions and now has left the office because the Department of Justice asked him and many others to investigate Renee Good's wife instead of doing their jobs doing fraud. So what I want to know is when are you going to get down to the original footprint as promised to us? Uh we're continuing to work at that. Although those investigators uh will continue to stay there to get to the bottom of that fraud to make sure that those vulnerable people that rely on those programs actually get services from those federal dollars that are spent that it's not stolen by criminals and uh used by individuals to enrich themselves. >> Uh Secretary Chungli Tao, are you aware that agents bashed in the door of a US citizen among elder with no criminal record? I can't speak to the specifics of that case. >> Did you see the photos of that man being dragged out of his house in Crocs and in his underwear? >> Yes, I did. >> Do you believe that he was involved in fraud? >> I am do not know where that status of that investigation is. >> Are you aware that he is a son of a beloved nurse that treated our soldiers in Vietnam? >> I believe that we have laws in this country that need to be enforced and need to be applied equally. agent. Speaking of enforcing the laws and following the laws, did they have a judicial warrant when they ran through an American citizen's door? >> Our law enforcement officers follow the same pro protocols and procedures that all law enforcement. >> Are you aware that he tried to show his identification to show that he was American citizen and they didn't want to see it? >> Again, these officers um conduct themselves in processes. If something was done inaccurately, then certainly that we will make sure that we correct it and rectify it in the future. Are you aware that the person they were looking for was in prison and had been in prison for years? >> Yes, I am. >> That's a target. Yes, we do targeted operations going after and looking. >> Do you agree that it is unacceptable for your agents to ram into someone's door and drag someone out in their underwear and below zero temperatures when they have the wrong guy? Our officers conduct targeted operations and uh utilize the law processes that are given to you answer that you think that's wrong. >> They needed to identify that individual and that >> they couldn't identify him by looking at his identification. Instead, they had to drag him out, throw him in a car, and drive him around for an hour. How about pulling off offduty police officers, Madam Secretary? every single one of whom made clear who they were. They were people of color, offduty police officers. In Brooklyn Park, Minnesota, the chief described how one offduty officer, someone of color, a US citizen, was stopped and confronted by ICE agents with their guns drawn, demanding her proof of citizenship. As Chief Bruy said, "I wish I could tell you that this was an isolated incident, but other chiefs said it had happened to their own officers." Why were these officers stopped? >> We have thousands of law enforcement operations that we do every single day. Do you think they were stopped because they were murderers? Were they racially drug traffickers off our streets? When I look at these American families that have been victimized by criminals that we have removed from cities and from communities, I'm grateful for the work that our ICE officers do. And by your only focus >> defending stopping off I would like to hear you talk about some of the operations dragging a elder out of justice when you only talk about these situations like this that we are conducting uh and you don't talk about the good work that they do to protect people from being victimized by people that are in this country that want to conduct violent crimes against them or take advantage of them. uh that the laws need to apply to everyone and we're out there enforcing the law and the former prosecant have always worked with our police well but that's not what was going these these ICE agents were not following police procedures after the killings of Renee Good and Alex Prey when I spoke to Alex's parents they told me that you calling him a domestic terrorist this was directly from them the day after he was killed a a nurse in our VA, Alex, one of the most hurtful things they could ever imagine was said by you about their son. Do you have anything you want to say to Alex Freddy's Py's parents? >> We were relying in the hours after that incident that was so horrific um on information we were getting from the ground from our thing you wanted to say to the parents or to the family of Renee Good after you called them domestic terrorist. even imagine what they have gone through in the loss of their son and the loss of their family members. >> But how about specifically calling them domestic terrorists without any evidence of that? >> Sir, I ma'am, I did not call him a domestic terrorist. I said it appeared to be an incident of >> I think the parents saw it for what it was. Uh, after the killings, the federal government refused to cooperate with state law enforcement agents, blocked Minnesota investigators from accessing the scene. I know because I got directly calls from the mayor. I tried to call the DOJ. I tried to do everything I could because they were very worried about what was going to happen immediately, especially after Alex Py's death. Do you think that blocking local law enforcement from the scene of a shooting makes people safer? Yes or no? Actually, our HSI law enforcement officers risked their lives on that scene preserving evidence and keeping the violent riers away from the evidence so it could be preserved for the investigation. >> Seen Secretary No. >> Huh. Yes, I understand that. >> Alex Py's letter and after it became very >> You believe it is in the interest of justice to have a full and transparent investigation? >> Yes, the investigations being led by FBI and internal investigations as well into this incident. Will you commit to reversing the decision to cooperate with the Minnesota Bureau of Criminal Apprehension, a agency of seasoned professionals that the agents worked with directly? The agents FBI agents worked with directly to investigate after the shooting assassination of Speaker Hortman, after the killing of those two little kids in that church with the bullets going through those stained glass windows. In both incidents, our federal and local law enforcement work together and are working together on those investigations, but they have been blocked, the locals have, from being part of this. Why is that, Miss No? >> The FBI is leading this investigation, and uh I would leave that question for them to answer. >> Thank you.
